title: salix myrtillifolia var. pseudomyrsinites
intro: Salix myrtillifolia var. pseudomyrsinites, also known as the creeping willow, is a variety of the Salix myrtillifolia tree, characterized by its shrub-like growth habit and slender branches. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional European folk medicine, where it has been employed to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The creeping willow's bark and leaves have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the creeping willow include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions or taken internally to support overall health and well-being.
